message:
fix(cursor): use frame dimensions for cursor viewport to fix 16px alignment drift

On Linux, VA-API requires 16-pixel aligned frames, so the video frame
can be slightly larger than the ghostty surface (e.g. 2560x1264 vs
2560x1254). The cursor viewport CSS was using surface dims for its
object-fit:contain emulation, creating an aspect ratio mismatch with
the canvas content and causing cursor drift (~0.6% at the bottom).

Use video frame dimensions for the viewport aspect ratio so it matches
the canvas exactly. For cursor percentages, detect the encoder path:
fast path (surfW == frameW, direct copy + padding) uses frame dims;
downscale path (surfW != frameW, stretched to fill) uses surface dims.
Falls back to surface dims before the first video frame arrives.
